SEARCH
Module storm.core
Package st.orm.core.repository.impl

Class StreamSupport

java.lang.Object
st.orm.core.repository.impl.StreamSupport
public final class StreamSupport extends Object
Helper class for working with streams.
Since:
1.7

  • Method Details

    • chunked

      public static <X> Stream<List<X>> chunked(@Nonnull Stream<X> stream, int size)
      Generates a stream of slices, each containing a subset of elements from the original stream up to a specified size. This method is designed to facilitate batch processing of large streams by dividing the stream into smaller manageable slices, which can be processed independently.

      If the specified size is equal to Integer.MAX_VALUE, this method will return a single slice containing the original stream, effectively bypassing the slicing mechanism. This is useful for operations that can handle all elements at once without the need for batching.

      Type Parameters:
      X - the type of elements in the stream.
      Parameters:
      stream - the original stream of elements to be sliced.
      size - the maximum number of elements to include in each slice. If size is Integer.MAX_VALUE, only one slice will be returned.
      Returns:
      a stream of slices, where each slice contains up to size elements from the original stream.

    • partitioned

      public static <K, V> Stream<StreamSupport.Partition<K,V>> partitioned(@Nonnull Stream<V> stream, int size, @Nonnull Function<V,K> partitionFunction)
      Partitions a stream into keyed chunks of a fixed maximum size.

      Elements are grouped by partitionFunction. For each key, elements are accumulated into chunks of at most size elements. Chunks are emitted as soon as they reach size. When the input stream is exhausted, any remaining partial chunks are flushed.

      This overload does not limit the number of distinct keys (no overflow partition is used).

      Type Parameters:
      K - the partition key type.
      V - the element type.
      Parameters:
      stream - the input stream.
      size - the maximum number of elements per partition chunk.
      partitionFunction - function used to derive the partition key from an element.
      Returns:
      a stream of partitions.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if size <= 0.
      Since:
      1.7

    • partitioned

      public static <V, K> Stream<StreamSupport.Partition<K,V>> partitioned(@Nonnull Stream<V> stream, int size, @Nonnull Function<V,K> partitionFunction, int maxPartitions, @Nullable K overflowKey)
      Partitions a stream into keyed chunks of a fixed maximum size, while limiting the number of distinct partition keys and routing excess keys to a dedicated overflow partition.

      Elements are grouped by partitionFunction. At most maxPartitions distinct partition keys are allowed in total. One slot is always reserved for overflowKey, even if it is not encountered in the input stream. This means the maximum number of "normal" keys derived from partitionFunction is maxPartitions - 1.

      If a new element would introduce a new "normal" key beyond this limit, the element is assigned to overflowKey instead. If maxPartitions is 1, all elements are assigned to overflowKey.

      For each key, elements are accumulated into chunks of at most size elements. Chunks are emitted as soon as they reach size. When the input stream is exhausted, any remaining partial chunks are flushed.

      The encounter order of partitions is stable: keys are emitted in encounter order of their first appearance. The overflow key will appear at the point where it is first needed.

      Type Parameters:
      V - the element type.
      K - the partition key type.
      Parameters:
      stream - the input stream.
      size - the maximum number of elements per partition chunk.
      partitionFunction - function used to derive the partition key from an element.
      maxPartitions - the maximum number of distinct partition keys allowed in total, including the reserved overflow slot.
      overflowKey - the key used as a sink for elements whose original partition would exceed the configured limit.
      Returns:
      a stream of partitions.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if size <= 0 or maxPartitions <= 0.
      NullPointerException - if overflowKey is null and maxPartitions != Integer.MAX_VALUE.
      Since:
      1.7
